title: calonemorchis brumalis
intro: Calonemorchis brumalis, also known as the Winter-green Orchid, is a type of orchid native to tropical Asia, characterized by its small, white flowers and thick, fleshy roots. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The roots of the Calonemorchis brumalis have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the reduction of fever and the treatment of infections. Calonemorchis brumalis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be consumed as a tea or used in traditional remedies to promote respiratory health and well-being.
